title: baeckea umbellifera
intro: Baeckea umbellifera, also known as the native rosemary or blue leaf baeckea, is a shrub native to Australia with small white flowers and narrow blue-green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Baeckea umbellifera include reducing fever and relieving headaches, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
